start.line: Nine dollars, Artie. You. Owe. Me. Nine. Dollars.

options.text: I PAID that tab. I distinctly remember intending to.
What's this Trivia Night I keep hearing about?
Can I get a coffee to think about it?
Nine dollars. Right. I'm on it.

paid.line: You 'distinctly remember intending to.' Artie, that's not paying. That's the fantasy of paying. The ledger deals in the OTHER kind.

ledger.line: The ledger has never been wrong. The ledger is the one thing in this diner that has never once let me down, and it says Skint, comma, Artie, comma, NINE.

trivia.line: Trivia Night. Booth 4 hosts, three-dollar pot, and the whole neighbourhood pretends it doesn't care who wins. Everybody cares who wins. YOU should care — three dollars is a third of a tab.

options.text: I don't know a single fact.

facts.line: No, but you KNOW people who know things. That tall one who eats my bread. The sharp one in the booth. Rent-a-brain, Artie. It's practically the American way.

coffee.line: The coffee goes on the tab. The tab is nine dollars. So the coffee is, spiritually, the tenth dollar, and I won't do it to you. Sit. Scheme. Come back with money.
